title: The Fields Of Coleraine
content:
آ آ آ آ On the fields of Colï؟½raine thereï؟½ll be labour in vain
آ آ آ آ Before the Great Western is ended,
آ آ آ آ The nags will have toilï؟½d, and the silks will be soilï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ And the rails will require to be mended.

آ آ آ آ For the gullies are deep, and the uplands are steep,
آ آ آ آ And mud will of purls be the token,
آ آ آ آ And the tough stringy-bark, that invites us to lark,
آ آ آ آ With impunity may not be broken.

آ آ آ آ Though Ballaratï؟½s fast, and they say he can last,
آ آ آ آ And that may be granted hereafter,
آ آ آ آ Yet the judgeï؟½s decision to the Border division
آ آ آ آ Will bring neither shouting nor laughter.

آ آ آ آ And Blueskin, Iï؟½ve heard that he goes like a bird,
آ آ آ آ And Iï؟½m told that to back him would pay me;
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s a good bit of stuff, but not quite good enough,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Non licuit credere famae.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Alfred ought to be there, we all of us swear
آ آ آ آ By the blood of King Alfred, his sire;
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s not the real jam, by the blood of his dam,
آ آ آ آ So I shaï؟½nï؟½t put him down as a flyer.

آ آ آ آ Now, Hynam, my boy, I wish you great joy,
آ آ آ آ I know that when fresh you can jump, sir;
آ آ آ آ But youï؟½ll scarce be in clover, when youï؟½re ridden all over,
آ آ آ آ And punished from shoulder to rump, sir.

آ آ آ آ Archer goes like a shot, they can put on their pot,
آ آ آ آ And boil it to cover expenses;
آ آ آ آ Their pot will boil over, the run of his dover
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½ll never earn over big fences.

آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s a horse in the race, with a blaze on his face,
آ آ آ آ And we know he can gallop a docker!
آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s proved himself stout, of his speed thereï؟½s no doubt,
آ آ آ آ And his jumpingï؟½s according to Cocker.

آ آ آ آ When Hynamï؟½s outstrippï؟½d, and when Alfred is whippï؟½d,
آ آ آ آ To keep him in sight of the leaders,
آ آ آ آ While Blueskin runs true, but his backers look blue,
آ آ آ آ For his riderï؟½s at work with the bleeders;

آ آ آ آ When his carcase of beef brings ï؟½the bullockï؟½ to grief,
آ آ آ آ And the rush of the tartan is ended;
آ آ آ آ When Archerï؟½s in trouble, whoï؟½s that pulling double,
آ آ آ آ And taking his leaps unextended?

آ آ آ آ He wins all the way, and the rest, sweet, they say,
آ آ آ آ Is the smell of the newly-turned plough, friend,
آ آ آ آ But you smell it too close when it stops eyes and nose,
آ آ آ آ And you canï؟½t tell your horse from your cow, friend.
meaning: About: Adam Lindsay Gordon was an Australian poet, jockey and politician.
